generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T TARTARIC ACID. Tartaric acid, C 4 H 6 O 6 . Preparation , Solutions in water, freshly prepared. Authorities. 1 , Nenning, Praktisch Mittheilunger, 1827 (Jour. de la Soc. Gall., Mat. Med. Pura, vol. 4, p. 519; 2 , Journ. de Chim. Méd., 1838, p. 72 (ibid.); 3 , Husemann, Pflanz, p. 550; 4 , Times (Pharm. Journ., vol. 4, 1845, p. 370), William Watts, æt. twenty-four years, drank 2 ounces dissolved; 5 , Cattell, Brit. Journ. of Hom., vol. 11, p. 337; 6 , Alfred S. Taylor, M.D., Principles and Practice of Med. Jurisprudence, 1873, p. 230, a man, æt. twenty-four years, drank 1 ounce dissolved in 1/2 pint of warm water, death in nine days. HEAD. ► Dizziness (in one hour), [1] . FACE. ► His face instantly became as red as fire, and having exclaimed that he was poisoned, he became speechless; he never after left his bed, where he died on the died on the tenth day), [4] . ► The lips turn brown and blackish at their inner edge, [1] . ► Constant dryness of the lips; he is obliged to moisten them, [1] . ► (Burning at the upper lip, on a spot where a pimple had been cured), [1] . ► Burning of the lips (in a quarter of an hour), [1] . MOUTH. ► The teeth are set on edge, [1] . ► Brown and dry tongue , [2] . ► Pasty taste in the mouth, in the morning, which ceases after eating, [1] . THROAT. [10.] ► Burning sensation in throat and stomach as if on fire, [5] . ► Complained of a burning sensation in his throat and stomach, as though he had drunk oil of vitriol, and stated that he could compare it to nothing but being all on fire, [6] . STOMACH. ► Very urgent thirst , [2] . ► Eructations, [1] . ► Nausea (immediately), [1] . ► Vomiting continued till death, [6] . ► Repeated and almost continual vomiting , [2] . ► The matters vomited were of a deep-green color , [2] . ABDOMEN. ► Pain in the umbilical region , [2] . ► Pinching below the navel, with emission of flatulence; cold feeling in the abdomen for a quarter of an hour (in ten minutes), [1] . [20.] ► Pinching in the abdomen, [1] . STOOL. ► Many stools during the night , [2] . ► The fæces were of the color of coffee-grounds , [2] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► Hoarseness and scraping in the larynx, [1] . ► Respiration accelerated, then laborious and slow, [5] . ► Respiration difficult and slow, [3] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Very weak action of the heart, [3] . ► Feeble pulse , [2] . BACK. ► Sharp pain in the region of the loins , [2] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Paralysis of the thighs and legs, [2] . [30.] ► Tearing pain at the soles of the feet, near the heel, which prevents him from setting his foot on the ground, after luncheon, [1] . GENERALITIES. ► Convulsions just before death, [3] . ► Excessive weakness, [3] . ► In the evening he feels very tired, and can scarcely drag himself along , [2] . ► General bruised sensation, especially of the lower limbs, [1] . ► Amelioration in the open air, [1] . SLEEP. ► Yawning and stretching, [1] . ► Frequent yawning, [1] . FEVER. ► General cold feeling, in the evening in bed, [1] . CONDITIONS. ► Amelioration. ► ( In the open air ), The symptoms.
